title: ATAH MCP Binding (v0.8)
description: ATAH MCP Binding (v0.8): MCP tool definitions exposing ATAH operations. One of the v0.8 reference bindings; alternative or additional bindings may be defined in future. The protocol-level requirements that these tools surface are defined in the Core Protocol and Registry Operation Profile schemas.

tools.name: find_professional
tools.description: Component 1 (Discovery) — find a credentialled or established professional for a matter requiring human expertise. Required `request_intent` (`self`, `on_behalf_of_client`, `referral_partner_search`) gates which next-step components are available. Required `limit` (1-100) replaces v0.8.1 `shortlist_size`. Returns a candidate set with the full trusted partner data payload, presentation_disclosure that AI platforms MUST surface to the user, and protocol_version. Professionals with matching_status of compliance-pending, regulatory-suspended, or admin-suspended are excluded from results regardless of filters. For `request_intent: 'referral_partner_search'`, the candidate set excludes professionals the requester is already connected to via an active referral-connection. The input conforms to the ATAH query schema; the output conforms to the ATAH match-response schema.

presentation_disclosure.user_facing_disclosure: ATAH does not recommend or endorse any professional. This is a provenance-visible candidate set, ordered by stratified randomisation within transparent bands. AI platforms reordering this list MUST preserve the disclosure that ATAH applied non-preferential ordering.

tools.name: initiate_introduction
tools.description: Component 2 — initiate a consumer-self handoff. Returns a handoff_id and a handoff_access_token that subsequent state-changing calls (submit_stage2_data, submit_stage3_data, cancel_introduction) MUST present. handoff_id alone is not a bearer token (spec §11.5). Available only when the originating Discovery query had request_intent: 'self'; ATAH validates this server-side and returns unauthorized otherwise. The required `flow_variant` selects the Component 2 flow (off_protocol, contact_share, full_lifecycle); see spec §6.1. Returns 409 Conflict if the consumer already has an active introduction to this professional. For high-stakes categories (per `pre_handoff_freshness_window_days` on `profession-categories.json`), a Layer 3 pre-handoff freshness check runs before sending the introduction notification; if the relevant channel hasn't been verified within the recency window, ATAH issues a fresh verification challenge and waits up to 24 hours. On timeout, returns the `professional_contact_unverified` deferred-back status so the consumer's AI can choose another candidate or wait. Per spec §12A.3.

handoff_access_token.description: Opaque short-lived token. Required for subsequent state-changing tool calls. Treat as a credential; do not log.

tools.name: check_introduction_status
tools.description: Check the current status of an active introduction. Available to the original asserter platform and to authenticated AI platforms presenting a consumer reference matching the handoff record (spec §11.5). Does NOT return personal data — only state, history, and timestamps.

consumer_ref.description: Cross-platform check: the calling platform's consumer reference must match the handoff's consumer_ref.

tools.name: submit_stage2_data
tools.description: Submit Stage 2 pre-handoff check data after the professional has accepted Stage 1. Renamed from submit_stage2_consent because this tool transmits both consent and data. The structure of prehandoff_data depends on the pre-handoff check type required by the category (conflict_check, scope_confirmation, capacity_confirmation, or fee_alignment). Personal data lands in the transient encrypted vault (spec §11.6) and is retrieved by the professional through authenticated retrieval — it does not leave the vault via SMS or email.

vault_reference.description: Opaque reference to the transient vault entry holding the submitted data.

held_status.description: Present when the professional has a concurrent Stage 2 in flight. The submission is held until the prior submission resolves.

tools.name: submit_stage3_data
tools.description: Release consumer contact details to the selected professional after explicit Stage 3 consent. Renamed from submit_stage3_consent. Stored in the transient encrypted vault (spec §11.6) and retrieved by the professional through authenticated retrieval — it does not leave the vault via SMS or email.

input.matter_summary: Drafting a vendor-side master services agreement for a 12-month engagement.

tools.name: cancel_introduction
tools.description: Consumer cancellation of an active Component 2 introduction. Per spec §11.9, this serves withdrawal scenarios 1 and 2: scenario 1 (consumer withdrawal before data sharing) stops the handoff before vault data is shared; scenario 2 (consumer withdrawal after Stage 2 viewed) stops future processing while acknowledging the professional has already seen Stage 2 data. The `withdrawal_state` field on the handoff record is set accordingly. Audit metadata is retained per §11.8; `previous_state`, `resulting_state`, and `reason_code` are recorded on the audit event. Final lifecycle state is `consumer-cancelled`.

input.cancellation_reason: Consumer no longer requires the engagement.

tools.name: revoke_consent
tools.description: Consumer revocation of a previously-issued consent receipt. Per spec §11.9 scenario 3 (Consumer withdrawal after Stage 3 retrieval): any remaining vault payload covered by the revoked receipt is crypto-erased immediately; further actions under the receipt are prevented. ATAH cannot force deletion from the professional's systems where the contact details have already been retrieved — that obligation rests with the professional under their own privacy framework. The handoff's `withdrawal_state` becomes `consumer_withdrew_post_stage3_retrieval` (or the appropriate value if the receipt covered an earlier stage). Audit metadata, the consent-receipt integrity record, and state history are retained per §11.8. handoff_access_token is required when the receipt is associated with a handoff.

handoff_access_token.description: Required if the receipt is associated with a handoff; otherwise optional.

tools.name: get_consent_requirements
tools.description: Retrieve the consent requirements for a specific scope and category. Allows AI platforms to determine what consent text and data categories must be captured before issuing a consent receipt. Returns the canonical consent text for the current version so the asserting platform can show the consumer text matching the consent_text_version it then records.

output.consent_text: To run a conflict check, your name and any opposing party's name will be sent to the lawyer for the purpose of confirming there is no conflict of interest. The information will not be retained beyond the period needed for that check.

tools.name: report_introduction_outcome
tools.description: Report the outcome of an introduction when it concludes. The outcome_code values cover successful introductions, professional declines, consumer cancellations, no-response, and concern reports. The consumer-reported-concern outcome routes to the concern flag mechanism (spec §5.10) — admin-only visibility, professional notified with right of reply. ATAH does not adjudicate; serious matters route to the relevant regulatory or professional body.

input.outcome_notes: Engagement letter signed; matter is proceeding.

tools.name: propose_referral_connection
tools.description: Component 3 — propose a referral connection from the calling professional to a target professional. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority; this tool does not accept consent_receipt_id. The target professional must have looking_for_referral_partners: true at the time of the call. Persistent proposal with default six-month expiry; silent lapse with deletion if unresolved by expiry. Multiple proposals may be issued in a single call by listing several target_professional_ids; each produces a separate proposal record.

input.proposal_message: Frequent cross-referrals for cross-border M&A; would value a recorded ATAH connection.

proposals.proposal_message: Frequent cross-referrals for cross-border M&A; would value a recorded ATAH connection.

tools.name: list_inbound_referral_proposals
tools.description: Component 3 — list pending referral proposals targeting the calling professional. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: list_outbound_referral_proposals
tools.description: Component 3 — list pending referral proposals the calling professional has sent. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: respond_to_referral_proposal
tools.description: Component 3 — accept or decline an inbound referral proposal. On accept, ATAH creates a referral-connection record and deletes the proposal; on decline, the proposal record is deleted. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: withdraw_referral_proposal
tools.description: Component 3 — the proposing professional withdraws a pending proposal before resolution. The proposal record is deleted; the audit event records the withdrawal. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: list_my_referral_connections
tools.description: Component 3 — list active referral connections involving the calling professional. Connection records are kept for de-duplication of `request_intent: 'referral_partner_search'` Discovery only; they are not used as a competence or trust signal anywhere in the protocol. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: withdraw_referral_connection
tools.description: Component 3 — either party withdraws from an active referral connection; the connection record is deleted. After withdrawal the de-duplication exclusion no longer applies. Per F-4, governed by professional delegated authority.

tools.name: set_looking_for_referral_partners
tools.description: Component 3 — set the calling professional's `looking_for_referral_partners` toggle. When true, the professional is in the candidate pool for incoming Component 3 referral-partner Discovery and may receive `referral-proposal` records; when false, the professional is excluded from `request_intent: 'referral_partner_search'` results and cannot be the target of new proposals. Toggleable on and off at any time. Per F-4 / F1.2, governed by professional delegated authority. Updates to the same field via `PUT /v1/professionals/me` (or its REST equivalent) are also permitted.

tools.name: list_my_notification_channels_with_verification_status
tools.description: Component 12A — list the calling professional's notification channels with their verification status (last_verified timestamps, verification_status values). The professional's AI surfaces this so the professional can act on any pending challenges before Layer 2 escalation triggers a matching_status flip to contact_unverified. Returns the array shape from `NotificationChannel` $defs on `professional-identity-credentialled.schema.json` / `professional-identity-established.schema.json`. Per §11.8, `destination_reference` is HMAC of the destination, not plaintext.

items.description: Mirrors the NotificationChannel $defs on the professional identity schemas.

tools.name: confirm_notification_channel_verification
tools.description: Component 12A — confirm a verification challenge previously issued for a notification channel (Layer 1 periodic verification or Layer 3 pre-handoff freshness check). On successful confirmation, the channel's `last_verified` is updated to the current timestamp and `verification_status` flips to `verified`. Useful for MCP-push challenges where the professional's AI assistant acknowledges on behalf of the professional, and for any other channel where the AI flow can mediate the confirmation. Per spec §12A.4 anti-phishing controls, the confirmation surface displays the professional's atah_id and the challenge timestamp for verification before the AI confirms; the AI MUST surface this to the professional rather than auto-confirming silently.

tools.name: get_my_visibility_explanations
tools.description: Component-1-adjacent — professional-facing visibility-explanation view (rules-derived per spec §11A.4 / F-18). Mirrors REST `GET /v1/professionals/me/visibility-explanations`. The view explains which inclusion rules apply to the professional in this category/jurisdiction, which exclusion reason categories could apply, the professional's current band assignment (derived from `profession-categories.json` `band_definitions`, NOT from observed query traffic), and the implementation's published ordering policy. **MUST NOT expose actual query-count data, query-history data, observed demand patterns, or any per-query information derived from consumer or platform traffic** — the view is rules-derived only. Authority basis: professional_delegated_token (or firm_delegation). Each retrieval is audit-logged. Implementations MAY defer the endpoint implementation to v0.8.3; the obligation itself is part of v0.8.2.

tools.name: withdraw_from_matching
tools.description: Professional withdrawal from matching — Component 2 withdrawal scenario 5 per spec §11.9. Sets the professional's matching_status to withdrawn; the professional is removed from inclusion in Discovery, but their record, required audit trail, dispute records, regulatory metadata, and existing Component 3 connections are RETAINED per §11.8. **High-impact action**: this tool MUST require step-up authentication at the point of action — re-auth via OAuth flow, second factor via TOTP, out-of-band push approval, or equivalent. The protocol mandates the requirement; the specific mechanism is implementation choice in v0.8.2. The audit event records previous_state, resulting_state, reason_code, the principal-delegation context, and the auth_tier reflecting the step-up authentication applied.

step_up_token.description: Token or proof that step-up authentication was completed at the point of action. The protocol requires that step-up authentication happen; the specific token shape is implementation-specific in v0.8.2.

tools.name: submit_consent_receipt
tools.description: Submit a captured consent receipt to ATAH and receive a consent_receipt_id. The asserting platform constructs the structured receipt (per consent-receipt.schema.json), captures consumer consent through its own consent ceremony, and submits the receipt here. ATAH validates, computes the SHA-256 hash, computes the F-3 continuity-binding metadata (client_id, pseudonymous_consumer_ref HMAC, data_categories_hash for disclosure_consent), stores the hash and metadata, and returns the consent_receipt_id. Subsequent consenting tools (find_professional, initiate_introduction, submit_stage2_data, submit_stage3_data, report_introduction_outcome) take consent_receipt_id by reference. Per spec §4.10: consent_type is restricted to query_authorization and disclosure_consent — engagement_consent is excluded by design. Per F-4: Component 3 actions are NOT consenting actions in the consumer-consent sense and do not use this tool.
